tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P1] Rename builtin-extensions source + service dirs and update build cpSync
tasks.description:
Rename the in-repo source and service directories from `builtin-extensions` to `core-extensions`. The runtime `{dorkHome}/extensions/` discovery directory is UNCHANGED â€” only the in-repo source dirs are renamed.

Steps (in order):
1. Move source dir: `apps/server/src/builtin-extensions/` â†’ `apps/server/src/core-extensions/`. It currently holds `marketplace/` (`extension.json` id `marketplace` name "Dork Hub", `index.ts`, `server.ts`). Use `git mv` so history is preserved.
2. Move service dir: `apps/server/src/services/builtin-extensions/` â†’ `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/`. It currently holds `ensure-marketplace.ts` and `__tests__/`. Use `git mv`.
3. Update the build step in `apps/server/package.json:14`. The current line is:
   `"build": "tsc && node -e \"require('fs').cpSync('src/builtin-extensions','dist/builtin-extensions',{recursive:true,filter:p=>!p.endsWith('.ts')})\"",`
   Change BOTH dir names to `core-extensions`:
   `"build": "tsc && node -e \"require('fs').cpSync('src/core-extensions','dist/core-extensions',{recursive:true,filter:p=>!p.endsWith('.ts')})\"",`
4. Grep the whole `apps/server/src/` tree for any remaining references to `builtin-extensions` (import paths, string literals, comments) and update them. Known call site: `apps/server/src/index.ts:37` imports `ensureBuiltinMarketplaceExtension` from `'./services/builtin-extensions/ensure-marketplace.js'` â€” this import path is fixed in task 1.2 when the file is renamed, but the directory portion must resolve.

Edge cases / notes:
- This task is purely structural (rename + path fixups). The actual `ensureCoreExtensions()` rewrite and `index.ts` wiring change happen in task 1.2.
- `dist/builtin-extensions` artifacts may exist from prior builds; a clean build will regenerate `dist/core-extensions`.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `apps/server/src/core-extensions/marketplace/` exists; `apps/server/src/builtin-extensions/` is gone.
- [ ] `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/` exists (with `ensure-marketplace.ts` + `__tests__/` for now); `apps/server/src/services/builtin-extensions/` is gone.
- [ ] `apps/server/package.json` build line references `src/core-extensions` and `dist/core-extensions`.
- [ ] `grep -rn 'builtin-extensions' apps/server/src` returns no results (the directory-name references; the `ensureBuiltinMarketplaceExtension` symbol is renamed in 1.2).
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server build` succeeds and copies `dist/core-extensions/marketplace/extension.json`.
- [ ] Run `pnpm test -- --run` for the server package (note `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`: bare `pnpm vitest run` falsely fails two DEV-env tests; build packages before bare vitest in the worktree).
tasks.activeForm: Renaming builtin-extensions directories and updating the build cpSync step

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P1] Generalize ensure-marketplace into ensureCoreExtensions() scanner and wire into index.ts
tasks.description:
Replace the one-off `ensure-marketplace.ts` (`ensureBuiltinMarketplaceExtension`) with a directory scanner that version-stages every bundled core extension and returns their tier metadata. Behavior-preserving: Dork Hub (`marketplace`) still stages and stays enabled.

Create `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/ensure-core-extensions.ts` exporting `ensureCoreExtensions(dorkHome): Promise<CoreExtensionInfo[]>`:

```typescript
const CORE_SOURCE_DIR = path.resolve(__dirname, '../../core-extensions');

/** Stage every bundled core extension into {dorkHome}/extensions/<id>/; return their tier metadata. */
export async function ensureCoreExtensions(dorkHome: string): Promise<CoreExtensionInfo[]> {
  // for each subdir of CORE_SOURCE_DIR with a valid extension.json:
  //   compare bundled vs installed version â†’ fresh-copy / upgrade-copy / no-op (unchanged logic)
  //   collect { id, defaultEnabled: manifest.defaultEnabled !== false, canDisable: manifest.canDisable !== false }
}
```

Implementation details:
- Reuse the EXISTING read-manifest-version + `fs.cp` copy logic from `ensure-marketplace.ts` verbatim per-extension (read bundled manifest version, compare to installed version at `{dorkHome}/extensions/<id>/extension.json`, copy tree on fresh-install or upgrade, no-op when versions match). Do not change the copy mechanics â€” only loop it over each subdir of `CORE_SOURCE_DIR`.
- Scan `CORE_SOURCE_DIR` (`path.resolve(__dirname, '../../core-extensions')`) for subdirectories; skip any subdir lacking a valid `extension.json`.
- For each staged extension collect `{ id, defaultEnabled: manifest.defaultEnabled !== false, canDisable: manifest.canDisable !== false }` and return the array.
- The `CoreExtensionInfo` interface (`{ id: string; defaultEnabled: boolean; canDisable: boolean }`) is defined/consumed in the resolution helper (task 3.3). For phase 1, define it locally in this file (or import it once 3.3 lands) â€” it must match `{ id: string; defaultEnabled: boolean; canDisable: boolean }`.
- `manifest.defaultEnabled` / `manifest.canDisable` are optional manifest fields added in phase 2 (task 2.3). In phase 1 they are absent, so `!== false` yields `true` for both â€” preserving today's "Dork Hub stages and stays on" behavior.

Wire into `apps/server/src/index.ts`:
- Remove the import at line 37: `import { ensureBuiltinMarketplaceExtension } from './services/builtin-extensions/ensure-marketplace.js';` and replace with `import { ensureCoreExtensions } from './services/core-extensions/ensure-core-extensions.js';`.
- Replace the call at line ~167 `await ensureBuiltinMarketplaceExtension(dorkHome);` with `const coreExtensions = await ensureCoreExtensions(dorkHome);` keeping the SAME non-fatal try/catch wrapper.
- Capture the returned `CoreExtensionInfo[]` and pass it to the `ExtensionManager` (constructor arg or a `setCoreExtensions()` setter). NOTE: the manager wiring that consumes this list lands in phase 3 (task 3.4/3.5); in phase 1, capture the value and pass it through if the constructor already accepts it, otherwise leave a clear single-line comment marking where it will be consumed. Do not drop the value.
- Delete `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/ensure-marketplace.ts` once the new file is in place and the import is repointed.

Error handling / edge cases:
- Staging stays NON-FATAL: a copy failure for one extension must not crash startup (same try/catch posture as today). Log and continue.
- A subdir without a valid `extension.json` is skipped silently (not staged, not returned).

Tests â€” migrate and generalize `ensure-marketplace.test.ts` â†’ `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/__tests__/ensure-core-extensions.test.ts`:
- Multi-extension scan (more than one subdir under `CORE_SOURCE_DIR`).
- Fresh-install / upgrade / no-op per extension (carry over the existing version-diff cases).
- Returns correct `CoreExtensionInfo[]` (`id`, `defaultEnabled`, `canDisable` derived via `!== false`).
- Idempotent on repeat calls (second call is a no-op when versions match).

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/ensure-core-extensions.ts` exports `ensureCoreExtensions(dorkHome): Promise<CoreExtensionInfo[]>` scanning `CORE_SOURCE_DIR`.
- [ ] `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/ensure-marketplace.ts` is deleted.
- [ ] `apps/server/src/index.ts` calls `ensureCoreExtensions(dorkHome)` in a non-fatal try/catch and captures the return value; no remaining reference to `ensureBuiltinMarketplaceExtension`.
- [ ] `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/__tests__/ensure-core-extensions.test.ts` exists with the multi-ext scan / fresh / upgrade / no-op / returns-info / idempotent cases, all passing.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes for the server package (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Generalizing ensure-marketplace into the ensureCoreExtensions scanner

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P2] Extend extensions config schema with deviation-list disabled[] field
tasks.description:
Extend the `extensions` block in the config schema to support deviation lists: the existing `enabled` (things turned ON that default OFF) plus a new `disabled` (things turned OFF that default ON). Backward-compatible and additive.

Edit `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts` (~line 135 â€” currently `extensions: { enabled: string[] }`). Replace with:

```typescript
extensions: z
  .object({
    /** Extension IDs the user turned ON that default OFF (user/marketplace + default-off core). */
    enabled: z.array(z.string()).default(() => []),
    /** Extension IDs the user turned OFF that default ON (default-on core). */
    disabled: z.array(z.string()).default(() => []),
  })
  .default(() => ({ enabled: [], disabled: [] })),
```

Mental model to preserve in comments: both lists record DEVIATIONS from each extension's default state. `enabled` records overrides for things whose default is off; `disabled` records overrides for things whose default is on. This is the JetBrains `disabled_plugins.txt` model generalized to two defaults.

Follow the `.claude/skills/adding-config-fields/` lifecycle for the schema portion:
- Verify the `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS` parse check still passes with the new shape (an empty/default config must parse to `{ enabled: [], disabled: [] }`).
- The conf migration is a separate task (2.2); the docs updates are folded into phase 6 (task 6.2). Add the field and defaults here, plus the upgrade-path test placeholder via task 2.2.

Edge cases:
- Both inner arrays AND the outer object carry `.default(() => [])` / `.default(() => ({ enabled: [], disabled: [] }))` so a config missing `extensions` entirely, or missing either inner key, parses cleanly.

Tests:
- Add/extend a schema test asserting: empty config â†’ `{ enabled: [], disabled: [] }`; a config with only `enabled` populated â†’ `disabled` defaults to `[]`; both lists round-trip.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `packages/shared/src/config-schema.ts` `extensions` is the two-list object above with all three `.default()` calls.
- [ ] `USER_CONFIG_DEFAULTS` parses to `extensions: { enabled: [], disabled: [] }`.
- [ ] Schema test covers empty / enabled-only / both-populated cases.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes for `@dorkos/shared` and downstream consumers compile (the config-manager and discovery consume this type).
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Extending the extensions config schema with the disabled deviation list

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P2] Add version-keyed config migration backfilling extensions.disabled
tasks.description:
Append a version-keyed migration to `CONFIG_MIGRATIONS` that backfills `extensions.disabled: []` for pre-existing configs that predate task 2.1. The user's `~/.dork/config.json` already exists in production, so this must be additive and must never remove `enabled`.

Edit `apps/server/src/services/core/config-manager.ts` (`CONFIG_MIGRATIONS` ~line 81). Append an entry:

```typescript
// version key resolved at release time â€” /system:release detects config drift and scaffolds the keyed migration at tag time
'<next-release>': (store) => {
  const ext = store.get('extensions');
  if (ext && !Array.isArray((ext as { disabled?: unknown }).disabled)) {
    store.set('extensions', { ...ext, disabled: [] });
  }
},
```

Important notes on the version key:
- Use the literal placeholder `'<next-release>'` and add a clear comment (above) noting it must be replaced with the actual release version at tag time, and that `/system:release` detects this drift and scaffolds/fills the keyed migration. Do NOT hardcode a real version â€” `projectVersion` derives from `SERVER_VERSION` (dynamic, never hardcoded) and the migration key is resolved at release.
- The migration is append-only; do not modify existing entries.
- The corrupt-recovery path reuses the same conf options, so this migration applies equally on recovery.

Follow the `.claude/skills/adding-config-fields/` lifecycle for the migration + test portion:
- Add an upgrade-path test to `apps/server/src/services/core/__tests__/config-manager.test.ts`: a prior config shaped `{ extensions: { enabled: ['foo'] } }` (no `disabled`) â†’ after migration gains `disabled: []` AND preserves `enabled: ['foo']`. Also assert a config with `disabled` already present is left untouched (idempotent).

Edge cases:
- A config with no `extensions` key at all is handled by the schema defaults (task 2.1), not this migration â€” the guard `if (ext && ...)` skips when `extensions` is absent.
- Only backfill when `disabled` is not already an array (guard against double-application).

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `CONFIG_MIGRATIONS` has a new placeholder-keyed entry (`'<next-release>'`) with the backfill body above and a TODO/comment explaining the release-time key resolution via `/system:release`.
- [ ] `config-manager.test.ts` upgrade-path test: prior config without `extensions.disabled` gains `[]`; `enabled` preserved; pre-migrated config untouched.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the version-keyed config migration to backfill extensions.disabled

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P2] Add defaultEnabled + canDisable optional fields to ExtensionManifestSchema
tasks.description:
Add two optional fields to the extension manifest schema. Both are harmless for user/marketplace extensions (ignored). `extension.json` is a DorkOS-internal manifest (not the Claude-Code `marketplace.json`), so adding fields carries no marketplace-format-compat concern.

Edit `packages/extension-api/src/manifest-schema.ts` (`ExtensionManifestSchema`). Add:

```typescript
/** For core extensions: whether this ships enabled. Omitted/true = on, false = off. Ignored for user extensions. */
defaultEnabled: z.boolean().optional(),
/** Whether the user may disable this extension. Defaults to true. false = always on, no toggle shown. */
canDisable: z.boolean().optional(),
```

Notes:
- Both are `.optional()` (no `.default()`) â€” consumers derive the effective value via `manifest.defaultEnabled !== false` and `manifest.canDisable !== false` (omitted â†’ treated as `true`). This matches the `ensureCoreExtensions()` derivation in task 1.2.
- Ensure the inferred TypeScript type (`ExtensionManifest`) picks up both optional fields and re-exports cleanly from the package barrel.

Tests â€” add a manifest schema test (in the extension-api package's test dir, alongside existing manifest-schema tests):
- `defaultEnabled` / `canDisable` parse when present (true and false).
- Both omitted â†’ manifest still valid; fields are `undefined`.
- Round-trip: a manifest with `defaultEnabled: false, canDisable: true` parses and retains those values.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `ExtensionManifestSchema` includes `defaultEnabled: z.boolean().optional()` and `canDisable: z.boolean().optional()` with the documenting comments.
- [ ] The inferred `ExtensionManifest` type exposes both optional fields; package builds/typechecks.
- [ ] Manifest schema test covers present (true/false), omitted, and round-trip cases.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Adding defaultEnabled and canDisable fields to the manifest schema

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P3] Add origin field to ExtensionRecord, ExtensionRecordPublic, and toPublic()
tasks.description:
Add `origin: 'core' | 'user'` tracking to the extension record types and surface it to the client. Origin is derived from the startup staging set (the IDs `ensureCoreExtensions()` staged), not from manifest claims â€” VS Code's `isBuiltin`-by-location pattern, which can't be spoofed.

Edit `packages/extension-api/src/types.ts`:
- Add `origin: 'core' | 'user'` to `ExtensionRecord`.
- Add `origin: 'core' | 'user'` to `ExtensionRecordPublic`.

Edit `apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-manager-types.ts`:
- Include `origin` in the `toPublic()` mapping so it is surfaced to the client (copy `record.origin` through to the public shape).

Notes:
- This task only adds the field to the types and the `toPublic()` passthrough. The value is ASSIGNED during discovery (task 3.4: `rec.origin = coreIds.has(id) ? 'core' : 'user'`). Until 3.4 lands, the field exists on the type; discovery sets it.
- Keep the field required (not optional) on both record types â€” discovery always sets it.

Tests:
- Extend the `toPublic()` test (or `extension-manager-types` test) to assert `origin` is carried through from the internal record to the public record for both `'core'` and `'user'` values.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `ExtensionRecord` and `ExtensionRecordPublic` in `packages/extension-api/src/types.ts` both carry `origin: 'core' | 'user'`.
- [ ] `toPublic()` in `apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-manager-types.ts` copies `origin` to the public shape.
- [ ] Test asserts `origin` survives `toPublic()`.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes (discovery + manager will reference `origin`).
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the origin field to extension record types and toPublic

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P3] Create extension-enable-resolution pure helper (defaultsOn, isEnabled, setEnabled)
[core-extensions] [P3] Point CoreExtensionInfo type from ensureCoreExtensions to the shared resolution helper
tasks.description:
Consolidate the `CoreExtensionInfo` type so `ensureCoreExtensions()` and the resolution helper share one canonical definition (avoids drift between the staging output and the resolution input).

Steps:
1. In `apps/server/src/services/core-extensions/ensure-core-extensions.ts` (task 1.2), remove any local `CoreExtensionInfo` definition and import it from `apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-enable-resolution.ts` (task 3.2): `import type { CoreExtensionInfo } from '../extensions/extension-enable-resolution.js';`.
2. Confirm `ensureCoreExtensions()` returns `Promise<CoreExtensionInfo[]>` with the exact shape `{ id, defaultEnabled, canDisable }` matching the canonical type.
3. Verify no second definition of `CoreExtensionInfo` remains in the server tree (grep).

Notes:
- This is a small consolidation task that exists because 1.2 (phase 1) and 3.2 (phase 3) both need the type. The canonical home is the resolution helper.
- If task 1.2 already imported from the resolution helper (because 3.2 landed first in the worktree branch), this task is a no-op verification.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `CoreExtensionInfo` is defined once (in `extension-enable-resolution.ts`) and imported by `ensure-core-extensions.ts`.
- [ ] `grep -rn 'interface CoreExtensionInfo' apps/server/src` returns exactly one result.
- [ ] `ensure-core-extensions.test.ts` still passes with the shared type.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` and `pnpm test -- --run` pass (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Consolidating the CoreExtensionInfo type into the resolution helper

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P3] Update discover() signature for tier-aware status + origin derivation
tasks.description:
Change discovery to take the full config + core info and set both `origin` and tier-aware `status` per record.

Edit `apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-discovery.ts`. The current `discover(cwd, enabledIds: string[])` (~line 31) becomes:

```typescript
async discover(
  cwd: string | null,
  config: ExtensionsConfig,
  core: Map<string, CoreExtensionInfo>,
): Promise<ExtensionRecord[]>
```

Import `ExtensionsConfig`, `CoreExtensionInfo`, and `isEnabled` from `./extension-enable-resolution.js` (task 3.2).

In the status-resolution loop (~lines 50â€“70), after the compatibility checks, for each record:
- Set `rec.origin = core.has(rec.id) ? 'core' : 'user'`.
- Set `rec.status = isEnabled(rec.id, config, core) ? 'enabled' : 'disabled'` (replacing the old `enabledIds.includes(rec.id)` membership check).
- Add `origin` to the discovery log line for observability (the log that records each discovered extension's id/scope/status now also logs `origin`).

Edge case (local override of a core id): a local `.dork/extensions/<id>` override of a core id is a dev-only edge case; `origin` remains `'core'` by id membership in the `core` map, using whichever code won the global/local merge. Do not special-case the merge â€” just key `origin` off `core.has(rec.id)` after the merge.

Tests â€” update `apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-discovery.test.ts`:
- New `discover(cwd, config, core)` signature (callers pass `{ enabled, disabled }` + a `Map`).
- Tier-aware status: a default-on core ext absent from `disabled` â†’ `'enabled'`; same ext in `disabled` â†’ `'disabled'`. A default-off core ext absent from `enabled` â†’ `'disabled'`; in `enabled` â†’ `'enabled'`. A user ext only enabled via `enabled`.
- `origin` derivation: ids in the `core` map â†’ `origin: 'core'`; others â†’ `origin: 'user'`.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `discover()` has the new three-arg signature and imports `isEnabled`/types from the resolution helper.
- [ ] Each record gets `rec.origin` (from `core` membership) and `rec.status` (from `isEnabled`).
- [ ] Discovery log line includes `origin`.
- [ ] `extension-discovery.test.ts` updated for the new signature, tier-aware status, and origin derivation; passing.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Updating discover() for tier-aware status and origin derivation

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P3] Route enable/disable through setEnabled, hold core map, add canDisable guard
tasks.description:
Make the manager tier-aware: hold the core map, pass the full config + core map to discovery on reload, and route toggles through the `setEnabled()` helper so the correct deviation list is mutated. Public API and return shapes are unchanged.

Edit `apps/server/src/services/extensions/extension-manager.ts`:

1. Hold `coreExtensions: Map<string, CoreExtensionInfo>` built from the `CoreExtensionInfo[]` passed in from `index.ts` (constructor arg or `setCoreExtensions(infos: CoreExtensionInfo[])` setter that builds the `Map` keyed by `id`). Import `CoreExtensionInfo`, `ExtensionsConfig`, `setEnabled` from `./extension-enable-resolution.js`.

2. `reload()` (~line 107): read the FULL `extensions` config via `configManager.get('extensions')` (now `{ enabled, disabled }`) and pass `{ enabled, disabled }` + the core map to `discover()`:
   `const config = configManager.get('extensions'); const records = await discovery.discover(cwd, config, this.coreExtensions);`

3. `enable()` (~line 198) and `disable()` (~line 227): replace the direct `enabled`-list mutation at lines 210â€“213 and 235â€“238 with the `setEnabled()` helper. Read current config, compute the next config, write it back:
   - `enable(id)`: `const next = setEnabled(id, true, configManager.get('extensions'), this.coreExtensions); configManager.set('extensions', next);` then reload/recompile as today.
   - `disable(id)`: `const next = setEnabled(id, false, configManager.get('extensions'), this.coreExtensions); configManager.set('extensions', next);` then reload/recompile as today.

4. `canDisable:false` guard in `disable()`: if the record's `origin === 'core'` and its `canDisable === false` (look up via the core map: `this.coreExtensions.get(id)?.canDisable === false`), `disable()` returns `null` (no-op) â€” defense in depth behind the UI. Do this guard BEFORE mutating config.

Wire from `index.ts`: ensure the `CoreExtensionInfo[]` captured in task 1.2 is now actually passed to the manager (constructor or setter) before `initialize()`/`reload()` runs. Complete the passthrough that task 1.2 stubbed.

Notes:
- Public API (`enable`/`disable` signatures, return shapes) and the HTTP route contract are unchanged â€” the server alone decides which list to mutate.
- `setEnabled` returns the whole `{ enabled, disabled }`, so write the entire object back, not just one list.

Tests â€” update `apps/server/src/services/extensions/__tests__/extension-manager.test.ts`:
- enable/disable route to the correct deviation list by origin/default: disabling a default-on core ext adds its id to `disabled` (not removes from `enabled`); enabling it removes from `disabled`. Enabling a default-off core ext or a user ext adds to `enabled`; disabling removes from `enabled`.
- `canDisable:false` disable is a no-op: returns `null`, config unchanged.
- reload passes `{ enabled, disabled }` + core map to discover (verify via the discovery mock/spy).

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Manager holds `coreExtensions: Map<string, CoreExtensionInfo>` (from index.ts).
- [ ] `reload()` passes `{ enabled, disabled }` + core map to `discover()`.
- [ ] `enable()`/`disable()` call `setEnabled()` and write the full `{ enabled, disabled }` back; old direct `enabled`-only mutation (lines 210â€“213, 235â€“238) removed.
- [ ] `disable()` returns `null` for `canDisable:false` core extensions before any config mutation.
- [ ] `index.ts` passes the staged `CoreExtensionInfo[]` to the manager.
- [ ] `extension-manager.test.ts` updated for correct-list routing + canDisable no-op; passing.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Routing enable/disable through setEnabled and adding the canDisable guard

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P3] Verify extension HTTP routes still flip state under the new model
tasks.description:
Confirm the extension HTTP API surface is unchanged and still works under the new deviation-list model. The endpoints `POST /api/extensions/:id/enable` and `POST /api/extensions/:id/disable` are untouched â€” the server (manager) decides which list to mutate, so the route handlers in `apps/server/src/routes/extensions.ts` need no signature change.

Steps:
1. Review `apps/server/src/routes/extensions.ts` enable/disable handlers â€” confirm they delegate to `extensionManager.enable(id)` / `extensionManager.disable(id)` and do not themselves touch `config.extensions.enabled`. If any route handler reads or mutates the config lists directly, refactor it to delegate to the manager (it should already).
2. No new endpoints, no contract change.

Tests â€” update `apps/server/src/routes/__tests__/extension-routes.test.ts` (or wherever extension route tests live):
- The unchanged endpoints still flip state correctly under the new model: `POST .../enable` on a default-on core ext that was disabled removes it from `disabled`; `POST .../disable` on it adds it to `disabled`. `POST .../enable` on a user/default-off ext adds to `enabled`; `.../disable` removes from `enabled`.
- `POST .../disable` on a `canDisable:false` core ext is a no-op (route returns the appropriate response; manager returned `null`).

Notes:
- This task is mostly verification + test updates; expect minimal-to-no source change in the route file. If the route returns a 200/204 today on disable, keep that behavior; if `disable()` now returns `null` for locked extensions, ensure the route surfaces a sensible response (e.g. the unchanged record or a clear status) without erroring.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `apps/server/src/routes/extensions.ts` enable/disable endpoints are unchanged in contract and delegate to the manager.
- [ ] `extension-routes.test.ts` asserts endpoints still flip state correctly (default-on core via `disabled`; default-off/user via `enabled`) and that `canDisable:false` disable is a no-op.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Verifying extension HTTP routes still flip state under the new model

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P4] Add origin to the client extension type
tasks.description:
Surface `origin` on the client-facing extension type so the settings UI can partition by tier.

Edit `apps/client/src/layers/features/extensions/model/types.ts`:
- Add `origin: 'core' | 'user'` to the client `LoadedExtension` (the client record type that mirrors `ExtensionRecordPublic`). The server already includes `origin` in `toPublic()` (task 3.1), so this is a type alignment so the field is typed/accessible in the UI.

Notes:
- `api/queries.ts` is UNCHANGED â€” `useExtensions` (GET, 30s poll), `useEnableExtension`/`useDisableExtension` (POST + invalidate) need no changes; the server decides which list to mutate so the HTTP surface (`POST /api/extensions/:id/{enable,disable}`) is untouched.
- Keep the field required (matches the server's required `origin`).

Tests:
- If a type/parse test exists for the client extension model, extend it to expect `origin`. Otherwise this is covered transitively by the `ExtensionsSettingsTab.test.tsx` (task 4.2) which depends on `origin`.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] Client `LoadedExtension` type in `model/types.ts` carries `origin: 'core' | 'user'`.
- [ ] `api/queries.ts` is unchanged.
- [ ] `pnpm typecheck` passes for `@dorkos/client`.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Adding origin to the client extension type

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P4] Partition ExtensionsSettingsTab into Core and Installed sections
tasks.description:
Split the flat extensions list into two labeled sections partitioned by `origin`.

Edit `apps/client/src/layers/features/extensions/ui/ExtensionsSettingsTab.tsx` (currently a flat `extensions.map`, ~lines 75â€“83). Partition the extensions by `origin`:
- "Core extensions" section: `extensions.filter(e => e.origin === 'core')` â€” Dork Hub (on), Hello World (off), Linear Loop (off) once phase 5 lands. This section always has members.
- "Installed extensions" section: `extensions.filter(e => e.origin === 'user')` â€” anything the user added. Empty by default: render a short empty-state with a pointer to Dork Hub (e.g. "No extensions installed yet. Browse Dork Hub to add some.").

Each section renders `ExtensionCard` per extension (the card changes land in task 4.3). Use clear section headers/labels consistent with the existing settings design system (study the surrounding Settings tab markup for heading style; do not invent a new pattern â€” follow established conventions per the codebase-excellence standard).

Notes:
- Maintain existing sort/order within each section (manifest/discovery order unless an explicit order is already used). No `CORE_EXTENSION_ORDER` list is required for the initial set.
- Keep loading/error states for the `useExtensions` query intact.
- Responsive by default â€” the two-section layout must work on mobile, tablet, and desktop (per the quality standard).

Tests â€” new `apps/client/src/layers/features/extensions/ui/__tests__/ExtensionsSettingsTab.test.tsx` (use mock `Transport` via `TransportProvider` and the testing patterns in `.claude/rules/testing.md`):
- Two sections render with the correct headers ("Core extensions", "Installed extensions").
- Partition by `origin`: a core-origin extension appears under Core; a user-origin extension under Installed.
- Empty Installed section shows the empty-state pointer when no user extensions exist.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `ExtensionsSettingsTab.tsx` renders "Core extensions" and "Installed extensions" sections, partitioned by `origin`.
- [ ] Empty Installed section shows an empty-state with a Dork Hub pointer.
- [ ] `ExtensionsSettingsTab.test.tsx` asserts two sections, correct partition by origin, and the empty-state.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Partitioning the settings tab into Core and Installed sections

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P4] Handle canDisable:false in ExtensionCard and keep health badge distinct from toggle
tasks.description:
Make `ExtensionCard` honor the `canDisable` flag and keep the runtime-health badge visually distinct from the on/off toggle.

Edit `apps/client/src/layers/features/extensions/ui/ExtensionCard.tsx`:
- When `manifest.canDisable === false`, render the toggle as locked/absent (no interactive switch) with a small "Required" hint instead of the normal toggle. Otherwise render the normal on/off toggle (existing behavior).
- Keep the health/availability badge (compile error / runtime error / unavailable) VISUALLY DISTINCT from the on/off toggle â€” these communicate different things (research pitfall #4: "disabled by user" is the toggle state; "unavailable due to runtime error" is the health badge). Do not conflate a runtime-error state with the off state of the toggle.

Notes:
- `canDisable` comes from the extension's manifest (surfaced through the public record). For the initial core set every extension has `canDisable: true`, so the locked path is exercised primarily by tests; build it now so the reserved flag works when used.
- Follow the existing card design system (badges, switch component) â€” do not introduce a new toggle/badge primitive.
- Disabling Dork Hub is allowed in the initial set (no locked core extension), so the normal toggle path remains the default.

Tests â€” add to `ExtensionsSettingsTab.test.tsx` (task 4.2) or a dedicated `ExtensionCard.test.tsx`:
- `canDisable: false` â†’ toggle is locked/absent and a "Required" hint shows.
- `canDisable: true` (or omitted) â†’ normal interactive toggle.
- A health/error state renders the badge distinctly from the toggle (badge present AND toggle present, not merged).

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `ExtensionCard.tsx` renders a locked/"Required" state when `canDisable === false`, normal toggle otherwise.
- [ ] Health/availability badge is rendered distinctly from the on/off toggle.
- [ ] Tests cover canDisable:false lock, normal toggle, and badge-vs-toggle separation.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Handling canDisable in ExtensionCard and separating the health badge

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P5] Set Dork Hub manifest defaultEnabled:true, canDisable:true
tasks.description:
Mark the Dork Hub (`marketplace`) core extension as default-on and disableable in its manifest.

Edit `apps/server/src/core-extensions/marketplace/extension.json` (the dir renamed in task 1.1):
- Add `"defaultEnabled": true` and `"canDisable": true`.

Notes:
- `defaultEnabled: true` could be omitted (the `!== false` derivation defaults to on), but set it explicitly for clarity and to make the curated tier self-documenting.
- This makes Dork Hub the default-on member that exercises the `disabled`-list opt-out path. A fresh user sees Dork Hub enabled; disabling it adds `marketplace` to `extensions.disabled`.
- Disabling Dork Hub is allowed â€” the `/marketplace` route already degrades gracefully when the extension is absent. No code change needed for that here.

Tests:
- Covered by `ensure-core-extensions.test.ts` (task 1.2/3.3) returning `{ id: 'marketplace', defaultEnabled: true, canDisable: true }`, and by discovery/manager tests for the default-on path. Add an assertion to `ensure-core-extensions.test.ts` for the marketplace entry's derived info if not already present.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `apps/server/src/core-extensions/marketplace/extension.json` has `defaultEnabled: true` and `canDisable: true`.
- [ ] `ensureCoreExtensions()` returns `{ id: 'marketplace', defaultEnabled: true, canDisable: true }`.
- [ ] A fresh-config user resolves Dork Hub to `enabled` (verified by discovery/manager tests).
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Setting Dork Hub manifest defaults to default-on and disableable

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P5] Move Hello World + Linear Loop into core-extensions as default-off, delete examples/extensions
tasks.description:
Relocate the two real extensions out of `examples/extensions/` into the core tier as default-off members, then delete `examples/extensions/` entirely.

Steps:
1. Move `examples/extensions/hello-world/` â†’ `apps/server/src/core-extensions/hello-world/` (use `git mv`). In its `extension.json`, add `"defaultEnabled": false` and `"canDisable": true`. (id `hello-world`, Name "Hello World".) Hello World doubles as the canonical authoring skeleton (its source is the reference) and a live toggleable demo.
2. Move `examples/extensions/linear-issues/` â†’ `apps/server/src/core-extensions/linear-issues/` (use `git mv`). In its `extension.json`, add `"defaultEnabled": false` and `"canDisable": true`. (id `linear-issues`, Name "Linear Loop", v2.0.0.) Linear Loop incubates here until `@dorkos/extension-api` is published and it migrates to the marketplace; it ships OFF by default.
3. Drop `examples/extensions/hello-world-js/` (not carried forward).
4. Delete `examples/extensions/` entirely after the two moves. If `examples/` becomes empty, leave the parent dir decision to whatever else lives there â€” only remove `examples/extensions/`.

Edge cases / safety:
- The copy-on-upgrade in `ensureCoreExtensions()` overwrites CODE files only; Linear Loop's Linear API key lives in the existing encrypted secret store, not in the staged code tree â€” staging never touches secrets (research pitfall #2). No data co-location.
- Both default-off members exercise the `enabled`-list opt-in path, proving that branch from day one.
- Confirm the moved extensions have valid `extension.json` so `ensureCoreExtensions()` picks them up (a subdir without a valid manifest is skipped).
- Linear Loop declares `externalHosts: ["https://api.linear.app"]` and ships off â€” no new network surface for default users.

Tests:
- Extend `ensure-core-extensions.test.ts` (task 1.2/3.3) to assert all three core extensions are scanned and return correct info: `marketplace` (defaultEnabled true), `hello-world` (false), `linear-issues` (false). Since the test uses the real `CORE_SOURCE_DIR` or a fixture, ensure it reflects the three-extension set.
- Discovery test (task 3.4) should resolve `hello-world` and `linear-issues` to `'disabled'` for a fresh config (absent from `enabled`), and to `'enabled'` when added to `enabled`.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `apps/server/src/core-extensions/hello-world/extension.json` has `defaultEnabled: false`, `canDisable: true`.
- [ ] `apps/server/src/core-extensions/linear-issues/extension.json` has `defaultEnabled: false`, `canDisable: true`.
- [ ] `examples/extensions/` no longer exists; `hello-world-js` is dropped.
- [ ] `ensureCoreExtensions()` returns the three-extension set with correct derived info.
- [ ] `pnpm --filter @dorkos/server build` copies all three into `dist/core-extensions/`.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Moving Hello World and Linear Loop into core-extensions and deleting examples

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P6] Add config-load hand-edit warning for default-on core ids in enabled
tasks.description:
Add a cheap, honest-by-design guardrail: on config load/validation, if any DEFAULT-ON core extension id appears in `extensions.enabled`, log a one-line warning (that entry is a no-op; the user likely meant to remove it from `disabled`).

Where: this check needs the core map (which IDs are default-on), so the most natural place is right after `ensureCoreExtensions()` returns the `CoreExtensionInfo[]` and before/at the first manager reload â€” i.e. in `apps/server/src/index.ts` near the staging call site, or as a small helper invoked there. (The config-manager itself does not know about core defaults, so do not put it inside `config-manager.ts`.)

Logic:
- For each `info` in the staged `CoreExtensionInfo[]` where `info.defaultEnabled === true`: if `configManager.get('extensions').enabled.includes(info.id)`, log a single-line warning, e.g.:
  `logger.warn('Extension "%s" is default-on; listing it in extensions.enabled is a no-op. To disable it, add it to extensions.disabled instead.', info.id)`
- One line per offending id. Do not mutate config (just warn) â€” the resolution helper already ignores the redundant entry.

Notes:
- This is a no-op for the common case (most users never hand-edit). It only fires for hand-editors who put a default-on id in the wrong list (research pitfall #1).
- Use the project logger (`@dorkos/shared/logger` or the server's logger convention â€” match the surrounding code).

Tests:
- Unit test the warning helper: given a core map with a default-on id and a config whose `enabled` contains that id â†’ warning logged once. Given the same id only in `disabled` â†’ no warning. Given a default-off core id in `enabled` â†’ no warning (that is the correct opt-in path). Spy on the logger and restore it in `afterEach` (per testing rules â€” never leave console/logger mocks without cleanup).

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] A config-load warning fires once per default-on core id found in `extensions.enabled`, with a clear one-line message pointing to `extensions.disabled`.
- [ ] No warning for default-off core ids or for ids only in `disabled`.
- [ ] Config is not mutated by the warning.
- [ ] Warning helper has a unit test with logger spy + cleanup.
- [ ] `pnpm test -- --run` passes (heed `reference_vitest_dev_env_gotcha`; build packages before bare vitest).
tasks.activeForm: Adding the config-load hand-edit warning for misplaced default-on core ids

tasks.subject: [core-extensions] [P6] Rewrite extension-authoring + configuration docs for Core Extensions
tasks.description:
Update the documentation to match the now-real Core Extensions behavior and the new config model. This corrects existing drift (the docs currently describe a `stageBuiltinExtension()` method that does not exist and claim built-ins are "always enabled").

1. `contributing/extension-authoring.md`: rewrite the "Built-in Extensions" section as "Core Extensions".
   - Remove the phantom `extensionManager.stageBuiltinExtension()` reference and the "do not appear as user-togglable items / are always enabled" claim â€” neither is true.
   - Describe the real model: core extensions live in `apps/server/src/core-extensions/<id>/`, are staged at startup by `ensureCoreExtensions()` into `{dorkHome}/extensions/`, appear in Settings under "Core extensions" with working toggles, and declare `defaultEnabled` / `canDisable` in their `extension.json`.
   - Update the Quick Start: it currently says to copy `examples/â€¦` to `~/.dork/extensions/`. That folder is gone. Instead reference the shipped Hello World core extension (`apps/server/src/core-extensions/hello-world/`) as the canonical authoring skeleton, and the scaffolding flow for new user extensions.
   - Document `defaultEnabled` (omitted/true = ships on, false = ships off) and `canDisable` (default true; false = locked on, no toggle) manifest fields.

2. `contributing/configuration.md`: document the new `extensions.disabled` field and the deviation-list mental model â€” `enabled` records overrides for default-off extensions (opt-in); `disabled` records overrides for default-on extensions (opt-out). Note the hand-edit caveat (a default-on id in `enabled` is a no-op; use `disabled` to turn it off).

3. `docs/getting-started/configuration.mdx`: same â€” document `extensions.disabled` and the deviation-list model for end users (Fumadocs-rendered).

Notes:
- Historical spec docs that mention `examples/extensions/` are LEFT AS-IS (point-in-time records). Do not rewrite specs/research files.
- This completes the `adding-config-fields` lifecycle's docs step started in phase 2.
- Match the existing doc structure/voice (brand voice: confident, minimal, technical) â€” do not introduce hype language.

Acceptance criteria:
- [ ] `contributing/extension-authoring.md` "Built-in Extensions" section is rewritten as "Core Extensions" with the real staging model, no `stageBuiltinExtension`/"always enabled" claims, an updated Quick Start pointing at the Hello World core extension, and documented `defaultEnabled`/`canDisable` fields.
- [ ] `contributing/configuration.md` documents `extensions.disabled` + the deviation-list model + the hand-edit caveat.
- [ ] `docs/getting-started/configuration.mdx` documents `extensions.disabled` + the deviation-list model.
- [ ] No specs/research files were modified.
- [ ] `grep -rn 'stageBuiltinExtension' contributing docs` returns no results.
tasks.activeForm: Rewriting extension-authoring and configuration docs for Core Extensions
